author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: digging for worms

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: Counting on my fingers, I tried to capture a single moment of nature in just a handful of words.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: There was a slope behind Willow Elementary, down near the playground. In winter, the slope iced over and at recess the kids would slide down it—sometimes lying down, sometimes standing up—riding the frozen wave like they’d invented flight.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: Garden Park runs behind the school along Willow Drive and extends from Fairmount Heights to the boundary of Lakeside.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: In spring we used the field there for softball and kickball.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: Sometimes it was littered with broken pop bottles; we’d kick or toss the glass into the brush before we started.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: I chose flowers.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: tending my little patch of zinnias and marigolds

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: every bug, leaf, and weed

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: There was a feral cat, who for some unknown reason sat in the middle of the street halfway between my house and the school.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: This cat was a scrawny thing but had all the chutzpah and tenacity of a mountain lion.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: It was afraid of nothing and nobody.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: It would casually lie in the street not bothering to move; not even for the occasional car coming down the road.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: that tabby

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: the cat didn’t mess with us

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: the cat hunched its back, fur puffing out like a blowfish

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: that damn cat again—sitting in the middle of the street licking itself like it owned the whole neighborhood

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: The trees of fall had long since shed their multi-colored leaves and the bite of winter was upon us.

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: the cat would hide behind bushes or crouch on porches

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: It was a warm, sunny day

author: TheWinterofMySoul SusanEHughes
Quote: I was running around like a wild dog
